sections.title: Primeros pasos
sections.summary: Initial setup requires a working installation, an active panel, and a designated primary administrator before any other credential is enrolled.
key_points: Verify installation alignment and free door movement.
Confirm the panel wakes correctly.
Define the primary administrator first.
Recommended order: administrator, PIN, fingerprint, validation, settings, app pairing.

sections.title: Agregar un administrador
sections.summary: Administrator creation happens from the user-management menu and should leave at least one backup credential registered.
key_points: Menu route: Agregar usuario -> Agregar administrador.
Register at least one PIN and one fingerprint for the main administrator.
Validate access immediately after saving.

sections.title: Agregar un usuario
sections.summary: User enrollment follows the same menu family as administrator enrollment but must avoid privilege mistakes.
key_points: Menu route: Agregar usuario -> Agregar usuario.
Enroll one user at a time.
Confirm the user did not inherit administrator privileges by mistake.

sections.title: Registrar una contrasena o PIN
sections.summary: PIN registration is handled from the user-enrollment flow and must be tested immediately after creation.
key_points: PINs can be attached to either administrators or regular users.
Avoid obvious credentials.
Confirm the credential opens consistently.

sections.title: Registrar una huella
sections.summary: Fingerprint enrollment requires clean, dry contact and repeated capture cycles until the device confirms completion.
key_points: Capture the same finger repeatedly as prompted.
Test fingerprint access several times in a row.
Backup fingerprints are recommended for administrators.

sections.title: Cambiar el idioma
sections.summary: The documented language path uses numeric entry codes, but those codes still require real-hardware confirmation before publication as final truth.
key_points: Observed entry code: 888#.
Observed Spanish code: 3009#.
Both codes require hardware validation before final commercial publication.

sections.title: Conectar la cerradura a la aplicacion
sections.summary: The primary observed ecosystem is Smart Life or Tuya, with pairing handled from the add-device flow and category-specific lock entry points.
key_points: Smart Life is the primary Beslock presentation path.
Tuya or Tuya Smart remains the ecosystem reference.
Device category selection must match the real hardware family.

sections.title: Vincular por QR
sections.summary: QR onboarding is the strongest observed OEM path for Smart Life pairing, using the camera-based lock flow and an on-screen QR code.
key_points: Observed app path: Add device -> Camera&Lock -> Video Lock (Wi-Fi).
The phone QR is shown to the lock camera after network selection.
Brightness, distance, and lighting should be adjusted one variable at a time when pairing fails.

sections.title: Alta del dispositivo por Wi-Fi o EZ Mode
sections.summary: EZ Mode is an alternate recovery or fallback flow and should not be merged editorially with the QR sequence.
key_points: Observed app path: Camera&Lock -> Lock (Wi-Fi) -> Reset the device -> EZ Mode.
This path is a fallback, not the same workflow as QR pairing.
If distribution fails, switch mode or use the hotspot guidance shown by the app.

sections.title: Desbloqueo remoto y registros
sections.summary: The app supports visitor-triggered remote unlock, two-way talk context, and event-log review for security and support operations.
key_points: Remote unlock can be triggered from a visitor request.
The app can expose talk flow with the visitor.
Event logs include ring, illegal user, low battery, and unlock events.

sections.title: Gestion de miembros
sections.summary: Member management includes adding named members, opening each member record, and binding the generated user code to an unlock mode.
key_points: The user code is generated when the lock is added.
Member records are edited from the member information surface.
Unlock mode assignment happens inside the member detail flow.

sections.title: Passwords temporales
sections.summary: The observed OEM flow clearly supports one-time and unlimited temporary passwords, while additional temporary-password modes still require validation on commercial hardware.
key_points: One-time passwords are described as ten-digit values.
One-time passwords expire after six hours and can only be used once.
Unlimited passwords are explicitly visible in the OEM source.
Additional temporary-password types may exist but are not yet validated.

sections.title: Problemas frecuentes
sections.summary: The current troubleshooting baseline separates biometric failure from app pairing failure and keeps the remedy set practical and non-speculative.
key_points: Fingerprint issues: clean the finger, retry naturally, re-enroll if needed.
App issues: confirm power, pairing mode, category selection, and network details.
Avoid promising unsupported diagnostics until hardware validation exists.
